And the second question I wanted
to ask you about is you've made some very useful, uh,
suggestions around potential amendments to the, to the bill,
um, and one of them in particular,
we've heard a little bit about transparency in your 8.2,
you talk a little bit about that.
Um, and should there be some obligation on financial
institutions which are, um, denying services, uh,
or withdrawing services
to actually be quite clear about the reasons behind that
and that they are and,
and the financial justification for that as opposed
to any other justification?
So, two parts of the question.
Yeah. So look in the, in the first instance, I think, um,
you know, at the, at the moment we have, uh,
some broad national goals of, um, of going for growth,
uh, a sort of doubling of the export value of,
um, our primary sector.
It's currently sitting at a, uh, 60 billion,
uh, dollar industry.
And, um, over the next decade, growing that to a hundred,
$120 billion industry is really important
for New Zealand as a whole.
And so we want to make sure
that we are creating an environment
where there is confidence for investors
to be putting, um, money into that sector in order for it
to grow and achieve that kind of outcome.
So I think that's the big picture.
What we're saying is that some of the questions
that have been asked are only creating uncertainty.
It's not clear how they relate to actual risk
or growth of the sector.
And so in a sense, we're just wanting to make sure
that there is good democratic oversight to enable
that process to happen.
Um, recognizing that, you know, there will be differences
of views between, um, various parliaments,
but we should, um, at least set the groundwork
where there's confidence
for future investment in order to grow the sector.
Thanks, mark. That was a very clear answer.
I think we'll leave it there. Uh, members we're,
we're slightly over, but appreciate, uh, fed farmers, uh,
Mark Hooper and Oliver for your, um, submission today.
Cheers guys. Thank you, uh, members, that's the end
of our part today, I think,
unless you're involved in the subcommittee meeting,
I think at two o'clock this afternoon.
Um, but um, yeah, appreciate your time
and we'll see you in Wellington two tomorrow.
Thanks, Taran. And team. Alright, thanks Ryan. Thank you Mr.
